core_mechanism: a parameter-controlled switch in the criticality of a Hopf-type bifurcation (continuous/supercritical onset of a limit cycle turning into an abrupt/subcritical one, or vice versa, as a secondary control parameter is tuned), with the switch mediated by a canard explosion in which the limit-cycle amplitude grows continuously but extremely rapidly across a vanishingly narrow window of the primary parameter
brief_reason: The abstract centers on the change of criticality of the transition to oscillation from supercritical to subcritical Hopf bifurcation under a secondary parameter, occurring through a canard explosion that is 'continuous yet rapid,' which is the bare bifurcation-theoretic machinery independent of the aeroacoustic subject matter.
